---
title: "Week 14 - Visualizing the data"
subtitle: "CHC9012 | National Taiwan Normal University"
format: 
  revealjs:
    theme: serif
    transition: slide
    scrollable: true
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    width: 1280
    height: 720
---

## 🎯 Goals

**Visualize your results:**

::: {.fragment .fade-up}
-   What types of figures to select?
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-up}
-   How to plot multiple information in one figure?
:::


---

## 💻 Dataset and script

Please download the files for the content of Week 13, and place them in your `PreprocessingAnalysesR` folder.

::: {style="text-align: center; margin-top: 2em;"}

<a href="https://github.com/aymeric-collart/CHC9012/blob/main/course-materials/Files/_Internal/CHC9012_PCIbexDataPrepared.RData?raw=true" 
   style="display: inline-block; padding: 15px 30px; background-color: #447099; color: white; text-decoration: none; border-radius: 10px; font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 25px; box-shadow: 0 4px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.1);">
   📥 Download the dataset for the script
</a>

<a href="https://aymeric-collart.github.io/CHC9012/course-materials/Files/_Internal/DataVisualization.Rmd" 
   download="DataVisualization.Rmd" 
   style="display: inline-block; padding: 15px 30px; border: 2px solid #447099; color: #447099; text-decoration: none; border-radius: 10px; font-weight: bold;">
   📥 Download the R Markdown script
</a>

:::
::: {.fragment .fade-up}
<br>
💻 Please start `RStudio` and select the project called `PreprocessingAnalysesR`.
:::


# 👀 Part 1: Why using figures, and what kinds of figures?

## Why plotting the data?

**Why using a figure when we already have the table of results?**

<div class="pulse-box">
  ⏳ **Activity:** Please spend 10 minutes to brainstorm this question.
</div>

<style>
.pulse-box {
  background-color: #eef2f7;
  border-left: 5px solid #0d6efd;
  padding: 20px;
  margin-top: 40px;
  border-radius: 4px;
  font-size: 2.5rem; 
  font-weight: bold;
  text-align: center;
  animation: pulse-animation 2s infinite;
}

@keyframes pulse-animation {
  0% { transform: scale(1); box-shadow: 0 0 0 0 rgba(13, 110, 253, 0.4); }
  70% { transform: scale(1.02); box-shadow: 0 0 0 10px rgba(13, 110, 253, 0); }
  100% { transform: scale(1); box-shadow: 0 0 0 0 rgba(13, 110, 253, 0); }
}
</style>

::: {.fragment .fade-up}
<br>

-   **Nature of human cognition.** Figures = more attractive than tables and texts.

-   **Eyes sharper than we think.** It is sometimes easier to observe tendencies with a figure.
:::


# Introduction to most common types of figures in experimental linguistics

## Box Plots for *Data Distribution*

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="45%"}

::: {.fragment .fade-up .callout-note appearance="simple"}
###  What it does:
Visualizes the spread, median, quartiles, and outliers of a continuous numerical variable across different categorical groups.
:::

<br>

::: {.fragment .fade-up .callout-note appearance="simple"}
###  Core question
**How does data distribution compare between groups?** *(e.g., Which Iris species has the longest petals?)*
:::
:::

::: {.column width="55%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig-width: 6.5
#| fig-height: 5.5

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(iris, aes(x = Species, y = Petal.Length, fill = Species)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, width = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Pastel1") +
  labs(
    title = "Petal length distribution by species", 
    y = "Petal length (in cm)",
    x = "Species"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) + # Bumps text size up so it is readable on the projector
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18)
  )
```
:::

:::

---

## Bar plots for *categorical counts*

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="45%"}

::: {.fragment .fade-up .callout-note appearance="simple"}
###  What it does:
Displays counts or frequencies of distinct categories.
:::

<br>

::: {.fragment .fade-up .callout-note appearance="simple"}
###  Core question
Which categories are the most and least common in our dataset?
:::
:::

::: {.column width="55%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig-width: 6.5
#| fig-height: 5.5

ggplot(mpg, aes(x = class)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.85, width = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "Count of vehicles by class", x = "Vehicle type", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
```
:::

:::

---

## Line charts for *continuous measurement, generally over time*

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="45%"}

::: {.fragment .fade-up .callout-note appearance="simple"}
###  What it does:
Connects individual data points chronologically or sequentially to reveal trajectories, spikes, or downward dips.
:::

<br>

::: {.fragment .fade-up .callout-note appearance="simple"}
###  Core question
How is our metric moving over a continuous sequence?
:::
:::

::: {.column width="55%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig-width: 6.5
#| fig-height: 5.5

ggplot(economics, aes(x = date, y = psavert)) +
  geom_line(color = "firebrick", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(title = "Personal savings rate trend", x = "Year", y = "Savings rate (in %)") +
  theme_minimal()
```
:::

:::

---

## Scatter plots for *relationships & correlations*

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="45%"}

::: {.fragment .fade-up .callout-note appearance="simple"}
###  What it does:
Plots individual data points at the intersection of two continuous numeric variables to display positive, negative, or non-linear correlations.
:::

<br>

::: {.fragment .fade-up .callout-note appearance="simple"}
###  Core question
Does variable X increase, decrease, or remain unchanged as variable Y changes?
:::
:::

::: {.column width="55%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig-width: 6.5
#| fig-height: 5.5

ggplot(iris, aes(x = Sepal.Length, y = Sepal.Width, color = Species)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  labs(title = "Sepal length vs. Sepal width", x = "Length (in cm)", y = "Width (in cm)") +
  theme_minimal()
```
:::

:::

---

## Combined plots for *more complex data*

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="45%"}

::: {.fragment .fade-up .callout-note appearance="simple"}
###  What it does:
Plots two types of data at the same time.
<br><br>
Here, a **boxplot** with an additional **scatter plot**.
:::
:::

::: {.column width="55%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig-width: 6.5
#| fig-height: 5.5

ggplot(iris, aes(x = Species, y = Sepal.Length, fill = Species)) +
  # 1. Plot the underlying summary structural distribution
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.4, outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.5) + 
  
  # 2. Layer the raw data points on top (jittered slightly horizontally so they don't stack up)
  geom_jitter(aes(color = Species), width = 0.2, size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Sepal length by species",
    subtitle = "Combining box plots with jittered data points to avoid hiding sample distribution",
    y = "Sepal length (in cm)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```
:::

:::

# Part 2: A step-by-step guide to plot figures with R

## 2.1 Introduction to `ggplot2` and its spirit {background-color="#f8fafc" linear-gradient="135deg, #f8fafc 60%, #e2e8f0 100%"}

::: {.columns style="display: flex; align-items: center; min-height: 550px;"}

::: {.column width="65%" style="padding-right: 40px;"}
Why using the `ggplot2` package:

-  Full control of what we want to plot
-  Customization of the figure (legends, annotations, changing the theme and the colors, etc.)


<div style="font-size: 2rem; line-height: 1.6; color: #0f172a; border-left: 6px solid #3b82f6; padding-left: 25px; font-style: italic; font-weight: 500;">
The core spirit of `ggplot2` is that **any data visualization can be broken down into a series of independent layers stacked on top of one another.**
</div>
:::

::: {.column width="35%" .text-center}
<div style="background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.7); backdrop-filter: blur(10px); border: 1px solid rgba(226, 232, 240, 0.8); border-radius: 16px; padding: 40px; box-shadow: 0 20px 25px -5px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05), 0 10px 10px -5px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.02); display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center;">

![](https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/logo.png){width="180px" style="margin-bottom: 25px; filter: drop-shadow(0 10px 15px rgba(0,0,0,0.1));"}

<span style="font-size: 0.85rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 1.5px; color: #64748b; font-weight: 600; display: block; margin-bottom: 20px;">Official Tidyverse Reference</span>

[<i class="bi bi-download"></i> Download Cheatsheet (PDF)](https://rstudio.github.io/cheatsheets/data-visualization.pdf){.btn .btn-primary role="button" target="_blank" style="border-radius: 50px; padding: 12px 30px; font-weight: 700; font-size: 0.9rem; background-color: #1e3a8a; border-color: #1e3a8a; box-shadow: 0 4px 14px rgba(30,58,138,0.3);"}

</div>
:::

:::

```{r}
#| echo: false
load(file = "CHC9012_PCIbexDataPrepared.RData")
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(Rmisc)
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| include: false

## 2.1 Select the participants with a human-like behavior
PartMeanRT <- aggregate(RT ~ ID_1_TimeReceived,
                        data = data3,
                        FUN = mean)

data_OtherPart <- subset(PartMeanRT, RT > 1000)
data_OtherPart_FullData <- droplevels(subset(data3,
                                             ID_1_TimeReceived %in% data_OtherPart$ID_1_TimeReceived))

plot(data_OtherPart_FullData$RT ~ data_OtherPart_FullData$ID_1_TimeReceived, outline = FALSE)
plot(data_OtherPart_FullData$Value ~ data_OtherPart_FullData$Condition)

## 2.2 Remove outlier trials

### 2.2.1 Absolute values
data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute <- subset(data_OtherPart_FullData, RT > 200 & RT < 20000)

### 2.2.2 Relative values
# Calculate the mean per Participant and Condition
data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$m_rt <- ave(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$RT, 
                                         data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$ID_1_TimeReceived, 
                                         data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$Condition, 
                                         FUN = mean)

# 2. Calculate the SD per Participant and Condition
data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$sd_rt <- ave(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$RT, 
                                          data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$ID_1_TimeReceived, 
                                          data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$Condition, 
                                          FUN = sd)

# 3. Subset the data to remove outliers
data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative <- subset(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute, 
                                               RT >= (m_rt - 2.5 * sd_rt) & 
                                                 RT <= (m_rt + 2.5 * sd_rt))

### 2.2.3 Calculate the number of trials removed
FullData_NbTrials <- nrow(data_OtherPart_FullData)
TrimmedData_NbTrials <- nrow(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative)

# Number of trimmed trials
NbTrimmedTrials <- FullData_NbTrials - TrimmedData_NbTrials
NbTrimmedTrials

# Proportion (%)
NbTrimmedTrials*100/FullData_NbTrials
```

# Overview of the figure creation process with `ggplot2`


## Layer 1: Initialize
::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="45%"}

**Step 1: Initialize**
<br>
Create the empty canvas.
:::

::: {.column width="55%"}

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 5

ggplot(
  data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative
)
```
:::
:::

---

## Layer 2: Aesthetics

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="45%"}

**Step 2: Indicate what to plot**
<br>
In ggplot2 terms, this is called the 'aesthetics' (`aes()`).
:::

::: {.column width="55%"}

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 5

ggplot(
  data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative,
  aes(x = Condition, y = Value)
)
```
:::
:::

---

## Layer 3: Geometry

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="45%"}

**Step 3: Indicate how to plot**
<br>
This is the step where you decide the kind of plot you want to use.
:::

::: {.column width="55%"}

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 5

ggplot(
  data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative,
  aes(x = Condition, y = Value)
) + 
  geom_boxplot()
```
:::
:::

---

## Layer 4: Polish

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="45%"}

**Step 4: Polish the details**
<br>
Change the theme, the color, add a title (and subtitle), change the legend, the font style and size, etc.
:::

::: {.column width="55%"}

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 5

ggplot(
  data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative,
  aes(x = Condition, y = Value, fill = Condition)
) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "skyblue", width = 0.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Acceptability judgments by Grammaticality",
    x = "",
    y = "Rating (1-7 Likert Scale)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.5, 7.5), breaks = 1:7)
```
:::
:::

# Examples with codes: Acceptability results

## A. Load the libraries

```{r}
#| message: false
#| echo: true

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(Rmisc)
```

---

## B. Process to data cleaning (Same as Week 13)

```r
## 2.1 Select the participants with a human-like behavior
PartMeanRT <- aggregate(RT ~ ID_1_TimeReceived,
                        data = data3,
                        FUN = mean)

data_OtherPart <- subset(PartMeanRT, RT > 1000)
data_OtherPart_FullData <- droplevels(subset(data3,
                                             ID_1_TimeReceived %in% data_OtherPart$ID_1_TimeReceived))

plot(data_OtherPart_FullData$RT ~ data_OtherPart_FullData$ID_1_TimeReceived, outline = FALSE)
plot(data_OtherPart_FullData$Value ~ data_OtherPart_FullData$Condition)

## 2.2 Remove outlier trials

### 2.2.1 Absolute values

data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute <- subset(data_OtherPart_FullData, RT > 200 & RT < 20000)

### 2.2.2 Relative values

# Calculate the mean per Participant and Condition
data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$m_rt <- ave(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$RT, 
                                         data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$ID_1_TimeReceived, 
                                         data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$Condition, 
                                         FUN = mean)

# 2. Calculate the SD per Participant and Condition
data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$sd_rt <- ave(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$RT, 
                                          data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$ID_1_TimeReceived, 
                                          data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$Condition, 
                                          FUN = sd)

# 3. Subset the data to remove outliers
data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative <- subset(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute, 
                                               RT >= (m_rt - 2.5 * sd_rt) & 
                                                 RT <= (m_rt + 2.5 * sd_rt))

### 2.2.3 Calculate the number of trials removed

FullData_NbTrials <- nrow(data_OtherPart_FullData)
TrimmedData_NbTrials <- nrow(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative)

# Number of trimmed trials
NbTrimmedTrials <- FullData_NbTrials - TrimmedData_NbTrials
NbTrimmedTrials

# Proportion (%)
NbTrimmedTrials*100/FullData_NbTrials
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| results: 'hide'
#| fig-show: 'hide'
#| message: false
#| warning: false

## 2.1 Select the participants with a human-like behavior
PartMeanRT <- aggregate(RT ~ ID_1_TimeReceived,
                        data = data3,
                        FUN = mean)

data_OtherPart <- subset(PartMeanRT, RT > 1000)
data_OtherPart_FullData <- droplevels(subset(data3,
                                             ID_1_TimeReceived %in% data_OtherPart$ID_1_TimeReceived))

plot(data_OtherPart_FullData$RT ~ data_OtherPart_FullData$ID_1_TimeReceived, outline = FALSE)
plot(data_OtherPart_FullData$Value ~ data_OtherPart_FullData$Condition)

## 2.2 Remove outlier trials

### 2.2.1 Absolute values

data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute <- subset(data_OtherPart_FullData, RT > 200 & RT < 20000)

### 2.2.2 Relative values

# Calculate the mean per Participant and Condition
data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$m_rt <- ave(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$RT, 
                                         data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$ID_1_TimeReceived, 
                                         data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$Condition, 
                                         FUN = mean)

# 2. Calculate the SD per Participant and Condition
data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$sd_rt <- ave(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$RT, 
                                          data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$ID_1_TimeReceived, 
                                          data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute$Condition, 
                                          FUN = sd)

# 3. Subset the data to remove outliers
data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative <- subset(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsolute, 
                                               RT >= (m_rt - 2.5 * sd_rt) & 
                                                 RT <= (m_rt + 2.5 * sd_rt))

### 2.2.3 Calculate the number of trials removed

FullData_NbTrials <- nrow(data_OtherPart_FullData)
TrimmedData_NbTrials <- nrow(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative)

# Number of trimmed trials
NbTrimmedTrials <- FullData_NbTrials - TrimmedData_NbTrials
NbTrimmedTrials

# Proportion (%)
NbTrimmedTrials*100/FullData_NbTrials
```

## C. Create the canvas

::: {.columns style="display: flex; align-items: center; min-height: 550px;"}

::: {.column width="50%" style="padding-right: 5px;"}

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| fig-show: 'hide'
ggplot(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative)
```

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
ggplot(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative)
```
:::

:::

## D. Include what to plot (the "aesthetics")

::: {.columns style="display: flex; align-items: center; min-height: 550px;"}

::: {.column width="50%" style="padding-right: 5px;"}

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| fig-show: 'hide'

ggplot(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative,
       aes(x = Condition,
           y = Value))
```

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

```{r}
#| echo: false

ggplot(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative,
       aes(x = Condition,
           y = Value))
```

:::

:::

## E. Include how to plot

::: {.columns style="display: flex; align-items: center; min-height: 550px;"}

::: {.column width="50%" style="padding-right: 5px;"}

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| fig-show: 'hide'

ggplot(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative,
       aes(x = Condition,
           y = Value)) + 
  geom_boxplot()
```

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

```{r}
#| echo: false

ggplot(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative,
       aes(x = Condition,
           y = Value)) + 
  geom_boxplot()
```

:::

:::

## F. Polish the details

::: {.columns style="display: flex; align-items: center; min-height: 550px;"}

::: {.column width="50%" style="padding-right: 5px;"}

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| fig-show: 'hide'

ggplot(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative,
       aes(x = Condition,
           y = Value,
           fill = Condition)) + 
  geom_boxplot(fill = "skyblue",
               width = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Acceptability judgments by Grammaticality",
       x = "",
       y = "Rating (1-7 Likert Scale)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.5, 7.5),
    breaks = 1:7)
```

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false

ggplot(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative,
       aes(x = Condition,
           y = Value,
           fill = Condition)) + 
  geom_boxplot(fill = "skyblue",
               width = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Acceptability judgments by Grammaticality",
       x = "",
       y = "Rating (1-7 Likert Scale)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.5, 7.5),
    breaks = 1:7)
```

:::

:::

# Bar plots of the acceptability results

## A. Create a summary of the results

```{r}
#| echo: true
JudgSummary <- summarySE(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative,
                         measurevar = "Value",
                         groupvars = "Condition")

JudgSummary
```

## B. Create the canvas

::: {.columns style="display: flex; align-items: center; min-height: 550px;"}

::: {.column width="50%" style="padding-right: 5px;"}

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| fig-show: 'hide'
ggplot(JudgSummary)
```

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
ggplot(JudgSummary)
```
:::

:::

## C. Include what to plot (the "aesthetics")

::: {.columns style="display: flex; align-items: center; min-height: 550px;"}

::: {.column width="50%" style="padding-right: 5px;"}

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| fig-show: 'hide'

ggplot(JudgSummary,
       aes(x = Condition,
           y = Value,
           fill = Condition))
```

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

```{r}
#| echo: false

ggplot(JudgSummary,
       aes(x = Condition,
           y = Value,
           fill = Condition))
```

:::

:::

## D. Include how to plot

::: {.columns style="display: flex; align-items: center; min-height: 550px;"}

::: {.column width="50%" style="padding-right: 5px;"}

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| fig-show: 'hide'

ggplot(JudgSummary,
       aes(x = Condition,
           y = Value,
           fill = Condition)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5, alpha = 0.9)
```

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

```{r}
#| echo: false

ggplot(JudgSummary,
       aes(x = Condition,
           y = Value,
           fill = Condition)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5, alpha = 0.9)
```

:::

:::

## E. Polish the details

::: {.columns style="display: flex; align-items: center; min-height: 550px;"}

::: {.column width="50%" style="padding-right: 5px;"}

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| fig-show: 'hide'

ggplot(JudgSummary,
       aes(x = Condition,
           y = Value,
           fill = Condition)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Value - ci,
                    ymax = Value + ci), 
                width = 0.15,
                size = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(Value, 2)), 
            vjust = 4,
            color = "white",
            size = 5,
            fontface = "bold") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(1, 7),
                     breaks = 1:7,
                     oob = scales::rescale_none)
```

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false

ggplot(JudgSummary,
       aes(x = Condition,
           y = Value,
           fill = Condition)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Value - ci,
                    ymax = Value + ci), 
                width = 0.15,
                size = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(Value, 2)), 
            vjust = 4,
            color = "white",
            size = 5,
            fontface = "bold") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(1, 7),
                     breaks = 1:7,
                     oob = scales::rescale_none)
```

:::

:::

# Complex bar plots for the acceptability ratings

## A. Create a summary of the results

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
###  How many datasets?

-  **Bar plots** for the general pattern,
-  **Scatter plots** to visualize the results from each trial.

We need two types of summary datasets: (1) general pattern, and (2) by-trial results.
:::

```{r}
#| echo: true
JudgSummary <- summarySE(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative,
                         measurevar = "Value",
                         groupvars = "Condition")
JudgSummary

JudgSummaryItem <- summarySE(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative,
                         measurevar = "Value",
                         groupvars = c("Condition", "ItemNo"))
JudgSummaryItem
```

## B. Create the figure

::: {.columns style="display: flex; align-items: center; min-height: 550px;"}

::: {.column width="50%" style="padding-right: 5px;"}

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| fig-show: 'hide'
ggplot(JudgSummary,
       aes(x = Condition,
           y = Value,
           fill = Condition)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5, alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Value - ci,
                    ymax = Value + ci), 
                width = 0.15,
                size = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(Value, 2)), 
            vjust = 4,
            color = "black",
            size = 5,
            fontface = "bold") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(1, 7),
                     breaks = 1:7,
                     oob = scales::rescale_none) +
  geom_jitter(data = JudgSummaryItem,
              aes(color = Condition),
              width = 0.2,
              height = 0.1,
              alpha = 0.5,
              size = 2.5) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
ggplot(JudgSummary,
       aes(x = Condition,
           y = Value,
           fill = Condition)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5, alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Value - ci,
                    ymax = Value + ci), 
                width = 0.15,
                size = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(Value, 2)), 
            vjust = 4,
            color = "black",
            size = 5,
            fontface = "bold") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(1, 7),
                     breaks = 1:7,
                     oob = scales::rescale_none) +
  geom_jitter(data = JudgSummaryItem,
              aes(color = Condition),
              width = 0.2,
              height = 0.1,
              alpha = 0.5,
              size = 2.5) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```
:::

:::

# Reaction time: Box plots

::: {.columns style="display: flex; align-items: center; min-height: 550px;"}

::: {.column width="50%" style="padding-right: 5px;"}

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
No need to summarize the data with boxplots!
:::

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| fig-show: 'hide'
ggplot(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative,
       aes(x = Condition,
           y = RT,
           fill = Condition)) + 
  geom_boxplot(fill = "skyblue",
               width = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "RT by Grammaticality",
       x = "",
       y = "Reaction time (in millisecond)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 20000),
    breaks = seq(0, 20000, by = 4000))
```

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
ggplot(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative,
       aes(x = Condition,
           y = RT,
           fill = Condition)) + 
  geom_boxplot(fill = "skyblue",
               width = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "RT by Grammaticality",
       x = "",
       y = "Reaction time (in millisecond)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 20000),
    breaks = seq(0, 20000, by = 4000))
```
:::

:::

# Reaction time: Bar plots

::: {.columns style="display: flex; align-items: center; min-height: 550px;"}

::: {.column width="50%" style="padding-right: 5px;"}


```{r}
#| echo: true
#| fig-show: 'hide'
RTSummary <- summarySE(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative,
                       measurevar = "RT",
                       groupvars = "Condition")

ggplot(RTSummary,
       aes(x = Condition,
           y = RT,
           fill = Condition)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = RT - ci,
                    ymax = RT + ci), 
                width = 0.15,
                size = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(RT)), 
            vjust = 8,
            color = "white",
            size = 5,
            fontface = "bold") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(4000, 7000),
                     breaks = seq(4000, 7000, by = 1000),
                     oob = scales::rescale_none)
```

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

kable(RTSummary) |>
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed", "responsive"),
    full_width = TRUE,
    position = "center"
  )

ggplot(RTSummary,
       aes(x = Condition,
           y = RT,
           fill = Condition)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = RT - ci,
                    ymax = RT + ci), 
                width = 0.15,
                size = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(RT)), 
            vjust = 8,
            color = "white",
            size = 5,
            fontface = "bold") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(4000, 7000),
                     breaks = seq(4000, 7000, by = 1000),
                     oob = scales::rescale_none)
```
:::

:::


# Complex bar plots for the reaction time

## A. Create a summary of the results

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
###  How many datasets?

-  **Bar plots** for the general pattern,
-  **Scatter plots** to visualize the results from each trial.

We need two types of summary datasets: (1) general pattern, and (2) by-trial results.
:::

```{r}
#| echo: true
RTSummary <- summarySE(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative,
                       measurevar = "RT",
                       groupvars = "Condition")

RTSummaryItem <- summarySE(data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative,
                         measurevar = "RT",
                         groupvars = c("Condition", "ItemNo"))
```

## B. Create the figure

::: {.columns style="display: flex; align-items: center; min-height: 550px;"}

::: {.column width="50%" style="padding-right: 5px;"}

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| fig-show: 'hide'
ggplot(RTSummary,
       aes(x = Condition,
           y = RT,
           fill = Condition)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5, alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = RT - ci,
                    ymax = RT + ci), 
                width = 0.15,
                size = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(RT)), 
            vjust = 8,
            color = "black",
            size = 5,
            fontface = "bold") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(3000, 9000),
                     breaks = seq(3000, 9000, by = 2000),
                     oob = scales::rescale_none) +
  geom_jitter(data = RTSummaryItem,
              aes(color = Condition),
              width = 0.2,
              height = 0.1,
              alpha = 0.5,
              size = 2.5) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
ggplot(RTSummary,
       aes(x = Condition,
           y = RT,
           fill = Condition)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5, alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = RT - ci,
                    ymax = RT + ci), 
                width = 0.15,
                size = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(RT)), 
            vjust = 8,
            color = "black",
            size = 5,
            fontface = "bold") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(3000, 9000),
                     breaks = seq(3000, 9000, by = 2000),
                     oob = scales::rescale_none) +
  geom_jitter(data = RTSummaryItem,
              aes(color = Condition),
              width = 0.2,
              height = 0.1,
              alpha = 0.5,
              size = 2.5) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```
:::

:::

# Visualizing the background information

## A. Create a summary of the background information

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
###  Why summarizing?

Our dataset includes more than 20 observations per participants, meaning that the same type of information (e.g., the age and highest degree of Participant 1) appears more than 20 times. Therefore, we need to create a new dataset where "1 row = 1 participant".
:::

```{r}
#| echo: true
ParticipantBackground <- data_OtherPart_CleanAbsoluteRelative %>%
  distinct(ID_1_TimeReceived, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  select(ID_1_TimeReceived, UserAge, UserDegree)

ParticipantBackground$UserDegree <- factor(ParticipantBackground$UserDegree, 
  levels = c("High school", "BA", "MA", "Ph.D.") )

ParticipantBackground
```


## B. Plot the age of the participants

::: {.columns style="display: flex; align-items: center; min-height: 550px;"}

::: {.column width="50%" style="padding-right: 5px;"}

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| fig-show: 'hide'
ggplot(ParticipantBackground, aes(x = "", y = UserAge)) +
  geom_boxplot(
    width = 0.4, 
    fill = "#4a90e2",
    color = "black",
    alpha = 0.7
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "Age") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
ggplot(ParticipantBackground, aes(x = "", y = UserAge)) +
  geom_boxplot(
    width = 0.4, 
    fill = "#4a90e2",
    color = "black",
    alpha = 0.7
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "Age") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```
:::

:::


## C. Plot the highest degree of the participants

::: {.columns style="display: flex; align-items: center; min-height: 550px;"}

::: {.column width="50%" style="padding-right: 5px;"}

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| fig-show: 'hide'
ggplot(ParticipantBackground,
       aes(x = UserDegree,
           fill = UserDegree)) +
  geom_bar(width = 0.5,
           color = "black",
           alpha = 0.8,
           show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(stat = "count",
            aes(label = after_stat(count)),
            vjust = -0.5,
            size = 5) +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "Number of participants") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Pastel1") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15)))
```

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
ggplot(ParticipantBackground,
       aes(x = UserDegree,
           fill = UserDegree)) +
  geom_bar(width = 0.5,
           color = "black",
           alpha = 0.8,
           show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(stat = "count",
            aes(label = after_stat(count)),
            vjust = -0.5,
            size = 5) +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "Number of participants") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Pastel1") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15)))
```
:::

:::

## D. Plot the age and the highest degree of the participants at the same time

::: {.columns style="display: flex; align-items: center; min-height: 550px;"}

::: {.column width="50%" style="padding-right: 5px;"}

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| fig-show: 'hide'
ggplot(ParticipantBackground,
       aes(x = UserDegree,
           y = UserAge,
           fill = UserDegree)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1,
              height = 0,
              alpha = 0.5,
              size = 2.5,
              color = "dimgray") +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.4,
               alpha = 0.7,
               outlier.shape = NA) +
  labs(x = "Highest degree",
       y = "Age") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Pastel1") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
ggplot(ParticipantBackground,
       aes(x = UserDegree,
           y = UserAge,
           fill = UserDegree)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1,
              height = 0,
              alpha = 0.5,
              size = 2.5,
              color = "dimgray") +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.4,
               alpha = 0.7,
               outlier.shape = NA) +
  labs(x = "Highest degree",
       y = "Age") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Pastel1") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```
:::

:::

# 📝 Assignment for next week

::: {.callout-important icon="true"}

1.  Please annotate the Markdown file from this week, knit to a PDF file, and upload on Moodle!

2.  **Next week = Assistance for handling results.** Please have the Markdown document(s) to import your results, clean the dataset and explore/visualize the data ready!
:::
